headline: Matthew Rhys Resurrects Legendary British Journalism in BBC's 'Dragon Slayers'
lede: Emmy-winner Matthew Rhys will transform the groundbreaking career of Harold Evans into a high-stakes BBC drama exploring investigative journalism's most pivotal moments.
body:
The BBC is doubling down on prestige historical storytelling with 'Dragon Slayers', a riveting biographical drama centering on Harold Evans, the transformative Sunday Times editor who redefined investigative reporting in the latter half of the 20th century.

Rhys, known for nuanced character work in 'The Americans', brings his trademark intensity to portraying Evans, a journalist who challenged institutional power through meticulous reporting. The series will trace Evans' most consequential investigations, including landmark exposés on medical malpractice, industrial negligence, and government corruption.

Evans' legendary tenure at the Sunday Times represents a golden era of British journalism, when reporters wielded genuine societal influence. His team's systematic approach to long-form investigative reporting uncovered systemic failures that prompted meaningful legislative and social reforms.

Production details remain closely guarded, but industry insiders suggest the drama will span Evans' most impactful decades, from the 1960s through the 1980s. Rhys' casting signals the project's commitment to authenticity and dramatic depth, promising a comprehensive portrait of a journalistic icon.

The BBC continues its tradition of transforming complex historical narratives into compelling television, positioning 'Dragon Slayers' as a potential awards-season contender.
key_facts: Focuses on Sunday Times investigative work
